GENERAL.FURTHERINFORMATION: A ~r~person has been murdered~w~. Head to the scene to ~b~recover evidence~w~ with your team.
GENERAL.NOTIFICATION: ~b~Search~w~ the area and press ~y~{0}~w~ to recover evidence.
~g~Finished searching?~w~ ~n~~y~Hold {0}~w~ to resume speaking to the ~b~Forensics Officer~w~.
This is the identified ~r~suspect~w~.~n~Press ~y~Y~w~ to see this notification again~w~.

OUTCOME.FURTHERINFORMATION: Visit the ~o~suspect address~w~ and attempt to apprehend the ~r~suspect~w~.
Locate the ~r~suspect~w~ who's travelling ~y~on foot~w~ nearby.
Head to the ~r~emergency call~w~ and see if it is the ~y~same suspect~w~ as your murder case.
Catch up to the ~r~suspect vehicle~w~ that is hitting ~b~ALPR cameras~w~.
Head to the ~b~subway~w~ and locate the ~r~suspect~w~ before they escape.

EXTRA.NOTIFICATION: Speak to the ~b~Forensics Officer~w~ to begin the ~y~investigation~w~.

DIALOGUES.DIALOGUE: ~y~Forensics Officer:~w~ Officer. We’ve secured the immediate area. One confirmed deceased. No signs of medical intervention.
~b~You: ~w~Understood. What’s the initial assessment?
~y~Forensics Officer:~w~ Visible trauma suggests a deliberate attack. We won’t speculate further until evidence supports it.
~b~You: ~w~Scene contamination?
~y~Forensics Officer:~w~ Minimal so far. Camera tech is documenting the body. I want the wider area searched before anything shifts.
~b~You: ~w~You want a grid sweep?
~y~Forensics Officer:~w~ Correct. Start close to the body and expand outward. Anything out of place — weapons, fabric, personal items — bag it.
~b~You: ~w~Bring items back as I find them?
~y~Forensics Officer:~w~ Negative. Complete the sweep first. Once you’ve recovered everything you can find, bring it all back to me together. I’ll process it in one run from the van.
~b~You: ~w~Understood. Anything else?
~y~Forensics Officer:~w~ No confirmation the suspect’s gone. Keep your head up while you search. Let the evidence tell us what happened.
~b~You: ~w~Alright. I’ll conduct the full sweep and report back.
~y~Forensics Officer:~w~ Glad you’re here. This one’s not pretty. One body, multiple injuries. Someone meant this.
~b~You: ~w~Any idea how long they’ve been down?
~y~Forensics Officer:~w~ Too early to say. What matters is whatever was used is probably still out there — or close.
~b~You: ~w~You thinking the weapon’s nearby?
~y~Forensics Officer:~w~ Most offenders don’t carry souvenirs unless they panic. Start near the body and work outward. Slow and careful.
~b~You: ~w~What am I looking for specifically?
~y~Forensics Officer:~w~ Anything that doesn’t belong. Blade, blunt object, discarded clothing, dropped property. If it feels wrong, bag it — but finish the search before you come back.
~b~You: ~w~So complete the grid, then return everything at once?
~y~Forensics Officer:~w~ Exactly. Once you’ve recovered all potential evidence, bring it to me together. I’ll run a full field analysis from the van. If there’s usable DNA or trace, we move fast.
~b~You: ~w~Copy that. I’ll secure the entire scene first.
~y~Forensics Officer:~w~ Good. Thorough beats fast. Let’s give this victim a voice.
~b~You: ~w~Beginning the full search now.
~y~Forensics Officer:~w~ You've given me no evidence, so this murder investigation cannot continue.
~y~Forensics Officer:~w~ Why bother turning up if you aren't going to do a good job?
~y~Forensics Officer:~w~ Well this is exciting. Let me take a look at this evidence and tell you what I find.
~y~Forensics Officer:~w~ I've been able to lift a serial number from the pistol. I can see who this firearm is registered to on the system.
~y~Forensics Officer:~w~ This ID card was very helpful. Good find. I can tell you exactly who it belongs to.
~y~Forensics Officer:~w~ You made this tricky for me, but I have swabbed the water bottle. You wouldn't believe it - I've got a DNA match.
~y~Forensics Officer:~w~ Looking at the fibre on the balaclava, I was able to run a match on some hair that was caught. Let's find out who this is.
~y~Forensics Officer:~w~ You should never use a knife, they're covered in fingerprints. I can tell you who held this.
~y~Forensics Officer:~w~ Well this is a first! This watch left at the crime scene has some engravings on the back. I can make out a name.
~y~Forensics Officer:~w~ No mobile phone is uncrackable. There was a lot of information to go through but I have found who this phone is registered to.
~y~Forensics Officer:~w~ Hi Dispatch, it's Timothy Ifield calling through my job phone.
~y~Forensics Officer:~w~ Hi Dispatch, it's Roz Huntley calling through my job phone.
~y~Forensics Officer:~w~ I have shared some forensics with you. I need you to send through the details to the investigating officer on scene with me.
~y~Dispatch:~w~ All received at the lab. Sending these through now.
~y~Dispatch:~w~ We've got an address related to this individual. You need to check it out.
~y~Dispatch:~w~ We've managed to intercept the location of this individual. Get there now.
~y~Dispatch:~w~ We've had an emergency call come in, someone reportedly at gunpoint nearby. Crap, this could be the same person - get there now!
~y~Dispatch:~w~ I have an ALPR hit on the vehicle about 30 seconds ago. You need to apprehend them.
~y~Dispatch:~w~ It looks like this individual has used a subway ticket recently, they're on the move.
~y~Forensics Officer:~w~ I'm sorry officer, I couldn't link any of this evidence to an individual.
~y~Forensics Officer:~w~ Sometimes we can find evidence but the suspects have been smart: from wearing gloves to untraceable firearms.
~y ~Forensics Officer:~w~ I guess we'll cleanup the scene and I'll see you at the next murder investigation.
